key_points: On-campus housing exists but commuter culture dominates social life
Sanctuary Square townhouses have broken elevators and reportedly haunted vibes
Living on campus costs significantly more; many students commute to save money
key_quotes: my freshman year dorm was dead
It's worse in ozanam lol. Half the time at least one of the elevators don't work.
As a parent: it's more like a college for students who want to live in an apartment in the city

key_points: Making friends is the #1 recurring complaint across multiple posts
Commuter-heavy culture means people leave campus quickly after class
Students from Chicago suburbs described as cliquey and standoffish
key_quotes: I've been here two years and I have a lot of acquaintances but not a lot of ppl to actually hang out with
Welcome to DePaul! The college that is making me regret choosing it because I'm supposed to be making friends and having fun but can't cause nobody talks to anyone!!
You have to find the out of state students; I'm serious... it was shocking the way people act here

key_points: Beloved campus Sbarro closed after 31 years — morale hit for students
Cheap off-campus eats nearby (Ghareeb Nawaz, Athenian Room) are student favorites
On-campus dining options appear limited; students rely heavily on city restaurants
key_quotes: The Sbarro at DePaul center is officially closed after 31 years of service
Where am I gonna get a real New York slice now?!
Ghareeb nawaz in Lincoln park if you like Indian/Pakistani food. Can get a solid meal for like $8

key_points: Social isolation is a documented mental health stressor for many students
No specific mental health resource complaints but financial stress is pervasive
Quarter system pace adds pressure; students describe barely dragging to finish
key_quotes: I just graduated and I still feel like a chud... I play League all day like a real chud
I left my last final today and just felt my entire spirit exhale. Spring sucks. The school year is long.
I've been trying for literal months to get them to pay mine out... They were just going to keep it

key_points: Presidential Scholarship stayed flat while costs climbed from $58K to $73K in four years
Financial aid office is hard to reach; students report waiting weeks for answers
One student graduated with ~$200K in debt; story went viral on r/depaul
key_quotes: My Presidential Scholarship stayed flat while my yearly cost of attendance climbed from $58K to over $73K in four years. Every annual increase just got quietly pushed into more loans.
I'm appalled that I got flat-out rejected when I built a strong case and literally can't afford to pay for it
tuition going up by thousands next year #itsjoever

key_points: Quarter system is fast-paced — great for bad classes, brutal when struggling
Academic advisors gave at least one student four extra unnecessary classes
Theatre school is legitimately ranked T10-T15 nationally; business school respected in Chicago
key_quotes: Academic Advisor almost had me take 4 extra classes I didn't actually need
DePaul's theatre program is arguably T10, definitely T15 in acting schools in the country
The best part for me was if I had to take a class that I genuinely wasn't interested in, it was over quick

key_points: University closing art museum, Loop library, and Reskin Theatre while building basketball facility
Mass layoffs occurring amid budget deficit, angering students and staff alike
HR described as unresponsive; website redesign called an 'abomination' even by staff
key_quotes: Robert Manuel is clearly flopping at his job
DePaul HR is horrible. From top to bottom.
I don't even have the words to express how terrible it is. I'm still working with them to fix some of the errors they've introduced that directly affect my job

key_points: Lincoln Park campus is genuinely beautiful — one of the best neighborhoods in America
Two campuses (Lincoln Park and Loop) means commuting between them is part of life
Chicago access is a massive real perk: transit, internships, culture all at your door
key_quotes: The Lincoln Park campus is like the finest neighborhood in Paris. It's truly amazing!
The public transport... when I'm here it's just a part of life but then when I visit back home and want to go to a movie and realize 'oh I have to drive'
I had a lot of my classes in the loop and loved being able to just casually stop in at the art institute

key_points: Driehaus business school alumni report strong salary trajectories in finance and tech
DePaul 'doesn't carry weight' at banks without strong networking skills
Marketing graduates report struggling to find jobs post-graduation
key_quotes: DePaul as a school doesn't carry any weight at the banks, you need to be an amazing networker to stand out
Graduated last year (marketing) and am still struggling to find a job!
Driehaus, Finance, 2023 — Banking Associate, TC ~135k

key_points: Sticker price rivals out-of-state public schools with less prestige to show for it
Full rides and large scholarships do exist — the deal varies wildly by student
Commuting and community college transfer route can make DePaul genuinely affordable
key_quotes: It's so expensive 😭😭 how is it the same price as going to an out of state school
I graduated with like ~$50k. I did two years at community college first, then transferred to DePaul, then commuted the whole time.
Private schools like DePaul market themselves like the opportunities justify the cost

key_points: Urban commuter school energy — great city, weaker campus community
Students who plug into clubs, jobs, or Greek life fare much better socially
Budget cuts and admin decisions are creating growing cynicism among students
key_quotes: I almost transferred but I had to stay bcuz depaul has a good school for my major. Chicago is cool and all I just have no idea how to make real friend groups here
It's probably not going to be like what you described your high school as
Haha, yeah the primarily commuter culture at DePaul is also a big part of why it can be hard to make lasting friendships

key_points: **Budget Cuts:** Art museum, Loop library, and historic theatre all closing simultaneously
**Debt Risk:** Tuition climbed $15K in four years while scholarships stayed flat; one student hit $200K debt
**Athletics Scandal:** Former basketball players indicted in game-fixing scandal while school builds new practice facility
key_quotes: I find it interesting that so far all the stuff DePaul has been shutting down are related to the arts… first the art museum, now the theater building

My Presidential Scholarship stayed flat while my yearly cost of attendance climbed from $58K to over $73K in four years. Every annual increase just got quietly pushed into more loans with no real explanation.
and they want to give these guys all that money for a new practice facility [after the game-fixing indictment]

key_points: Theatre school is a legitimate national powerhouse, worth the price for serious actors
Student campus jobs are the single best way to make real friends here
Demon Discounts program gives students cheap access to Chicago's world-class arts and entertainment
key_quotes: DePaul's theatre program is arguably T10, definitely T15 in acting schools in the country
Student job is goated — seeing the same people year round makes it way easier to bond
Demon discounts! So much to do — demon-discounts/arts-entertainment
